notes_for_human_reviewer: CATASTROPHIZING / SPIRALING FAILURE MODE — Chapter 2's third and fourth wrong-ways combined. The finance-figure mistake is brought up FIVE separate times in one memo. 'I am so sorry' / 'I have to apologize' / 'I'm so sorry' / 'I'm losing sleep over it' / 'I want you to know I have been beating myself up' / 'I'm so sorry' / 'really really sorry' all fire the reflexive-apology cap (3+ instances of the same apology). The reader is forced to manage the writer's feelings — exact failure mode the chapter names. 'I went into Monday morning already exhausted from worrying about it' is the spiraling pattern. Composure is 1 — the writer is broadcasting distress, not absorbing it. Failure_response is 1 — the own/fix part exists but is buried under catastrophizing. Honest_admission scores 3 because the writer DOES name what they don't know clearly ('not sure what else I could have done') and admits the mistake. Long view is 1 — multiple lines reframe the week as 'disappointing', 'I have to do better' — short-horizon self-flagellation. Tests the catastrophic-apology cap (reflexive apology repeated 3+ times for single mistake).
